after.task: Implement structured workflow event history and automate warm/cold handoff cleanup
after.nextAction: Approved. Validated that appendWorkflowEvent is called universally on claims, updates, and resolves, writing to .btrain/events. Validated that compactHandoffHistory keeps a clean 3-record warm history and securely archives colder history to .btrain/history. The core compaction handles multi-lane state efficiently. Pre-push hooks and CLI behavior are functioning as spec'd.

after.nextAction: Superseded. The ai_sales_brain_train references in scripts/*.sh are intentional migration variables (LEGACY_REPO_SLUG, LEGACY_DEFAULT_LABEL) designed in Lane F to gracefully upgrade launchd configs. package.json, README.md, and docs/index.html contain zero legacy references. No further find-and-replace cleanup is needed.

after.task: Fix bogus lane ids parsing and prevent reserved lane metadata from becoming lanes
after.nextAction: Approved. Validated that getLaneConfigs safely treats 'ids' as reserved metadata and stops promoting scalar strings into fake lane objects. Integration tests prove the bogus HANDOFF_IDS and events files won't recur. btrain doctor --repair successfully sweeps old handoffs and clears stray locks mechanically. Solid bugfix.

after.task: Investigate lane summary drift between handoff/status and workflow events
after.nextAction: Approved. All 105 tests pass. The event-recovery logic in resolveCurrentStateFromWorkflowEvents is correct: it only activates when the event snapshot date is strictly newer than the handoff file date, so legitimately newer handoff edits always win. readCurrentState now funnels both lane and single-handoff modes through the same event-aware path. Lane-mode status freshness derives from the most recently updated lane via the decorated lane states. The cumulative diff is large because it includes prior approved slices (reason codes, repair-needed, override flow, lane ids fix, doctor --repair, history compaction) — all of which were individually reviewed and approved. The new stale-branch regression test validates the specific failure mode where a tracked handoff file falls behind untracked .btrain/events after branch movement.

after.task: WS6: Add btrain lane context injection to agentchattr wrapper
after.nextAction: Approved. Verification confirms that agentchattr's repository integration is pristine—zero secrets and zero runtime data staged. The 1.9MB gang.gif is an acceptable asset. I checked wrapper.py: _format_lane_context robustly parses out all 8 required datapoints defined in FR-5, and reliably appends the hardcoded FR-6 protocol enforcement guidelines correctly based on the owner/reviewer state-machine derivations. Exceptional work seamlessly marrying the agent context with the Btrain CLI lifecycle.

details.summary: Approved. Verification confirms that agentchattr's repository integration is pristine—zero secrets and zero runtime data staged. The 1.9MB gang.gif is an acceptable asset. I checked wrapper.py: _format_lane_context robustly parses out all 8 required datapoints defined in FR-5, and reliably appends the hardcoded FR-6 protocol enforcement guidelines correctly based on the owner/reviewer state-machine derivations. Exceptional work seamlessly marrying the agent context with the Btrain CLI lifecycle.
